footer.description:
This site serves as my portfolio and detailed work experience overview. It also includes a comprehensive list of my projects, which I have categorized and shortly described.
If you want to know more about me, my skills, or my work, this is the place to be.
footer.techStack: Technologies used to build this site

Here are some non-coding tools that I have experience using.
I use Arch, btw ;)

groups.management: Project management and version control systems

webDev.title: Web development
webDev.desc:
These are projects that I did in my free time.

I only want to share projects that I completed independently, so I'm not including work projects, as they are team efforts and don't accurately reflect my individual skills.

Please note that some of the projects listed on this page are outdated and based on obsolete technologies.
The tech stack I'm familiar with today is quite different and more modern.

in2rp.desc: Website created for GTA V role play server. It was integrated with discord bot and discord server dedicated to the GTA V role play community. Project had also database for storing server statistics and user data.

fivem.desc: It's not a website but an desktop application created with electron which allows developers to use web technologies for the UI part. FiveM is a popular GTA V modded server.

paradise.desc: Just a simple website for another GTA V roleplaying server. It was just a presentation homepage with some information about the server. There was a stunning parallax effect that looked like a sunset over the ocean.

mapPoi.desc: A project created as a recruiting challenge for my first job as a front-end developer. It was a simple map with POI markers loaded from given data. No map library was used in this project. All map interaction and rendering was done in code.

gameDev.title: Game development
gameDev.desc:
I've always wanted to make games.
Below are some examples of games I've made in my spare time.

berta.desc:
My biggest game project. Multiplayer browser game with custom graphics engine, advanced GLSL shaders and a very unique physics engine that basically allows any image with a transparent background to be used as an interactive game object that can be destroyed and painted over.
Some other features are paypal integration, real-time chat between players, ranking system, in-game currency with interactive shop and much more.

kulka.desc:
I made my first game in high school for a nationwide competition. Winning the competition gave me the confidence to pursue a career as a software developer.

Textures and all game assets were also made by me.

astro.desc:
My second game written in java and opengl. Made one year after Kulka w tarapatach. It has 3D graphics with custom GLSL shaders, 2D physics engine and advanced particles system.
There is also a map creator built in the game.

tetris.desc: It was more of a code challenge than game development. It's 700 lines of javascript code that anyone can paste and run in their browser console to see the game appear on top of existing content. It should work on any website. The idea was to include it as an easter egg in other of my projects.

tentactoe.desc1: Another code challenge, this time in HTML and JavaScript. It's an advanced version of Tic Tac Toe.
tentactoe.desc2: The game creates a peer-to-peer connection between players and allows them to play against each other. It works without a server, all data is exchanged directly between players. Basically, an index.html file can be opened locally and it will still work as a multiplayer game.

zerog.desc: Game created for university project. It's unique 2D graphics engine is based on svg. It has a 2D physics engine built from scratch, a complex map editor and it can be partially controlled by voice commands.

compGraphics.title: Computer graphics
compGraphics.desc: Computer graphics is also one of my passions that I can share here. I'm not a professional in this field, but I like to create some graphics from time to time.

blender.desc1: Blender is my software of choice for creating 3D renders. I've always been fascinated by photorealistic graphics, so I started learning techniques to create them.
blender.desc2: I also use GIMP for creating textures or some post-processing of my renders.
blender.desc3: Most of my renders with full quality can be found here:

rpi.title: Raspberry Pi
rpi.desc: My other computer-related hobby is building things with Raspberry Pi boards.

robot.desc:
Something I built to learn Raspberry PI.
It was a remote controlled robot on wheels with a camera streaming video to the website.

cyclocomputer.desc:
First version of my bike touring project. It is an e-paper display (very power efficient) connected to a Raspberry Pi that shows some handy info like current speed, wind direction, etc. It also displays a small map with a plotted route.
Everything is controlled and configured by a mobile app build with React Native.

biketour.desc:
New version of the Cyclocomputer project. This time it has an LCD screen and a camera that takes pictures every 500 meters.
Note: mobile app code is on separate branch in git repository

drone.desc:
My most ambitious project so far (not finished yet). A custom built drone controlled by Raspberry Pi via UART protocol. It is equipped with a GPS module, a raspberry pi camera and a mobile network module that allows access to the internet from almost anywhere in the world, giving the drone unlimited range.
There is also a web interface for controlling the drone and showing live stream from the camera and other sensors.

asystent.desc1: another university project. This time, it was a group effort
asystent.desc2: It was a fully voice-controlled music player that could control played audio and manage playlists. All of this was possible with predefined voice commands. It was created before LLM models became popular.

enigma.desc3: For most of the time, I was part of a team of front-end developers working on the project website. I gained valuable experience with modern web technologies.

games.desc: Fortunately, game development is possible solo so throughout my life I've turned a few ideas into game projects.

web.desc1: Before I was employed as a front-end developer, I created a few websites as a freelancer. I also worked on some full-stack projects for personal use, which I've included in the list below.

berta.title: Berta Snakes
berta.desc: My biggest game project so far. Multiplayer browser game with WebGL based graphics, custom shaders and very unique custom physics engine.

zerog.desc: 2D game with svg based graphics physics engine build from scratch.

tentactoe.title: Ten tac toe
tentactoe.desc: Mini project - a multiplayer game working without a server, based on peer-to-peer (p2p) communication.

in2rp.title: In2rp
in2rp.desc: Homepage created for a GTA V role-play server. The site was integrated with a Discord bot and the server database.

paradise.title: Project Paradise
paradise.desc: Another homepage for different GTA V role-play server.

scraper.title: Web Scraper

genetic.title: Genetic Algorithm
genetic.desc: Experiments with a neural network evolving with a genetic algorithm. All coded from scratch

drone.title: Drone
drone.desc:
Custom-built drone that is connected to a Raspberry Pi, which enables all kinds of behaviors and custom routines.
Dron jest również połączony z kamerą i modemem sieci komórkowej, co pozwala na sterowanie i strumieniowanie wideo w czasie rzeczywistym z niemal dowolnej odległości.

bike.title: Bike tour assistant
bike.desc:
Raspberry Pi with a little LCD screen attached to my bike that displays current speed and mini navigation.
Cooperates with mobile app for route planning and GPS tracking.
